_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **COMPETITIVE EDGE** — Evaluate all owned, leased, or landlord-managed warehouse, cargo-handling, and office premises against BREEAM NC V7 eligibility. New Construction Version 7 registrations are now open on the new BREEAM Platform (launched 30 September 2025); registration under legacy BREEAM 2018 and V6 schemes closes **27 January 2026**. Any new facility construction or major fit-out contract entering design now must be scoped under V7 to avoid forced mid-project scheme migration. *Source: BRE Global, BREEAM NC V7 launch press release, 30 September 2025. https://bregroup.com/news/bre-launches-new-version-of-leading-sustainability-tool-breeam-new-construction*

- **ACTION REQUIRED** — Confirm with all landlords and real-estate partners for cargo hubs, bonded warehouses, and live-event logistics staging facilities (CA, GB, US, EU trade lanes) whether leased buildings are registered or certified under any BREEAM scheme. Existing certified buildings require BREEAM In-Use recertification every three years; expiring certificates represent a gap in investor and ESG disclosure chains. *Source: BREEAM / BRE Global, breeam.com/about/how-breeam-works, accessed May 2026.*

- **MONITORING** — Track the forthcoming BREEAM Refurbishment and Fit-Out V7 release (announced for early 2026) and BREEAM In-Use V7 (announced for later 2026). These schemes govern fit-out of existing cargo facilities and operational asset benchmarking — the most common workspace engagement point for a freight forwarder occupying third-party premises. No registration window has opened yet for RFO V7 or In-Use V7 as of May 2026. *Source: BRE Global / BusinessWire, 30 September 2025. https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250930244356/en/*

- **COMPETITIVE EDGE** — Map BREEAM V7 EU Taxonomy alignment capability into RFP responses and supplier sustainability questionnaires for European clients (particularly GB and EU cross-border lanes). BREEAM NC V7 contains optional EU Taxonomy-aligned criteria covering climate change mitigation, climate change adaptation, and circular economy objectives, enabling streamlined CSRD/EU Taxonomy disclosure evidence for shipper clients. *Source: BREEAM / BRE Global, breeam.com/about/disclosures-and-reporting/breeam-eu-taxonomy, accessed May 2026.*

- **COST ALERT** — Budget for BREEAM assessor engagement if the workspace or any joint-venture logistics partner is constructing or commissioning new facilities in GB, US, CA, or internationally. V7 introduces mandatory multi-stage Life Cycle Assessment (concept, technical design, as-built) under Mat 01, expanding scope to include MEP system embodied carbon — a materially broader evidence burden than V6. Early assessor appointment avoids costly design-stage rework. *Source: Atelier Ten, BREEAM V7 Key Changes, December 2025. https://www.atelierten.com/articles/breeam-v7-key-changes/*
